---
name: generate-traceability-matrix
description: Generate a traceability matrix mapping work items to acceptance criteria to code to tests. Use when asked to "generate a traceability matrix", "trace requirements to code", "verify AC coverage", or "show me what's tested".
di_signal: INTENT
maturity_required: early
status: approved
---

# Generate Traceability Matrix

## Overview
Generates a traceability matrix that maps work items → acceptance criteria →
code → tests, surfacing gaps at each link in the chain: code with no test
(Code Only), ACs with no code (NOT COVERED), and situations where data is
unavailable to assess (UNGRADED).

Traceability is the evidence layer that connects delivery decisions to stated
requirements. Without it, a passing build proves only that the tests pass —
not that the feature does what it was supposed to do.

## When to Use
- Before closing a sprint to verify all ACs are covered by code and tests
- Before a PR merge to confirm the diff addresses the linked work item's ACs
- When a QA engineer asks which tests cover a specific requirement
- When an auditor or compliance review requires a requirements-to-test trace
- When a defect escapes and the team needs to trace back to which AC lacked
  coverage
- Any time the user says: "generate a traceability matrix", "trace requirements
  to code", "verify AC coverage", "show me what's tested", "map stories to tests"

## Instructions

### Step 1: Read the Work Items and ACs
**From work item IDs:**
- Read the title, description, and ACs for each work item
- Note the traceability comment format in use (ADO: `// AB#XXXX`, Jira: `// PROJ-XXX`)

**From a description:**
- Accept the work item list and ACs
- Confirm the tracking system format

Ask for (if not provided):
- The file paths or directories to scan for traceability comments
- The test file locations (convention: `tests/`, `spec/`, `__tests__/`, etc.)

### Step 2: Search the Codebase for Code References
For each work item, search for:

1. **Traceability comments** in source files: `// AB#1234`, `// PROJ-123`, `// #456`
2. **Function/method names** that match AC keywords (e.g. AC says "cancel order"
   → search for `cancelOrder`, `cancel_order`, `CancelOrder`)
3. **New files** created in the relevant feature area (e.g. `wishlist/`, `payments/`)

For each code location found:
- Map it to the most likely AC it implements (by name matching or comment context)
- Note the file path and function name

If no code references found for a work item: status is NOT COVERED.
If the search cannot be completed (no file paths provided, codebase unreadable):
status is UNGRADED.

### Step 3: Search for Tests Covering That Code
For each code reference found, search for test coverage:

1. Look for test files that import or reference the code file
2. Look for test function names that reference the AC (e.g. "should cancel order")
3. Look for traceability comments in test files (a test stub generated by Dev.IQ
   carries `// TEST STUB — AB#XXXX`)

**Status rules:**
- Test file found with test function covering the AC → **Covered**
- Code found but no test file/function found → **Code Only**
- AC maps to no code in the codebase → **NOT COVERED**
- Data unavailable (test files not locatable, work item unreadable) → **UNGRADED**

Never fabricate coverage. If test files cannot be located: mark UNGRADED
and state the reason.

### Step 4: Produce the Matrix
Generate a row per AC, not per work item — each AC must be independently traceable.

Group rows by work item for readability.

### Step 5: Summarize Gaps and Recommend Actions
After the matrix, produce:
- Count of each status
- Gap list with recommended action per gap type
- For Code Only gaps: recommend Assert.IQ test generation
- For NOT COVERED gaps: raise as INTENT gap — code must be written or the AC revisited
- For UNGRADED: state what additional information would resolve the uncertainty

At **Early maturity**: include coaching notes on why each gap type matters.

At **Mid/Higher maturity**: structured output only.

## Inputs Required
| Input | Source | Required |
|-------|--------|----------|
| Work item ID(s) and ACs | Work item system or paste | Yes |
| Codebase file paths to scan | User provides or inferred | Yes |
| Test file locations | User provides or inferred from codebase conventions | Yes |
| Traceability comment format | `.dev-iq/config.yaml` or inferred | Auto-detected |
| Maturity tier | `.dev-iq/config.yaml` | Auto-read |

## Output Format

```
## Traceability Matrix — [Work Item ID(s) or Sprint Name]
Assessed: [date]
Work items: [list of IDs]
Code search scope: [directories searched]
Test search scope: [test directories searched]

---

### Matrix

| Work Item | AC | AC Description | Code File / Function | Test File / Test Name | Status |
|-----------|----|----|--------|---------------------|--------|
| AB#XXXX | AC1 | [AC description] | src/orders/OrderService.ts: processOrder() | tests/orders/OrderService.test.ts: should process valid order | Covered |
| AB#XXXX | AC2 | [AC description] | src/orders/OrderService.ts: cancelOrder() | — | Code Only |
| AB#XXXX | AC3 | [AC description] | — | — | NOT COVERED |
| AB#YYYY | AC1 | [AC description] | [could not locate] | [could not locate] | UNGRADED |

---

### Coverage Summary

| Status | Count | % of ACs |
|--------|-------|---------|
| Covered | [N] | [%] |
| Code Only | [N] | [%] |
| NOT COVERED | [N] | [%] |
| UNGRADED | [N] | [%] |

---

### Gap Analysis and Recommended Actions

**Code Only — [N] ACs**
Code exists but no tests found. Recommend Assert.IQ `/generate-tests` for:
- [AC reference] — [function name] in [file path]

**NOT COVERED — [N] ACs**
No code found for these ACs. This is an INTENT gap:
- [AC reference] — [AC description] — Action: implement the AC or mark it
  as deferred with a follow-up work item

**UNGRADED — [N] ACs**
Insufficient data to assess:
- [AC reference] — Reason: [test files not found / work item unreadable / etc.]
- Resolution: [what information would resolve the uncertainty]

---

### INTENT Signal: [STRONG | WEAK | UNGRADED]

@di-review-required
```

## Examples

### Example 1: Fully Covered Story

**User says:** `/generate-traceability-matrix` for AB#2202 (wishlist sharing)

**Result:**

```
## Traceability Matrix — AB#2202: Add wishlist sharing
Assessed: 2026-06-06
Code search scope: src/wishlists/, src/api/routes/
Test search scope: tests/wishlists/, tests/api/

---

### Matrix

| Work Item | AC | AC Description | Code File / Function | Test File / Test Name | Status |
|-----------|----|----|--------|---------------------|--------|
| AB#2202 | AC1 | Share link generated and copied to clipboard | src/wishlists/ShareableLink.ts: generate() | tests/wishlists/ShareableLink.test.ts: should generate a signed URL | Covered |
| AB#2202 | AC2 | Recipient views wishlist without logging in | src/api/routes/sharedWishlist.ts: getSharedWishlist() | tests/api/sharedWishlist.test.ts: should return wishlist for valid token | Covered |
| AB#2202 | AC3 | Expired/invalid link returns error message | src/wishlists/ShareableLink.ts: validate() | tests/wishlists/ShareableLink.test.ts: should return 404 for expired token | Covered |

---

### Coverage Summary

| Status | Count | % of ACs |
|--------|-------|---------|
| Covered | 3 | 100% |
| Code Only | 0 | 0% |
| NOT COVERED | 0 | 0% |
| UNGRADED | 0 | 0% |

---

### Gap Analysis
None. All ACs are fully traced from work item through code to tests.

---

### INTENT Signal: STRONG

@di-review-required
```

---

### Example 2: Story with Untested Code and Missing AC

**User says:** `/generate-traceability-matrix` for AB#3105 (payment integration)

**Result:**

```
## Traceability Matrix — AB#3105: Stripe payment integration
Assessed: 2026-06-06
Code search scope: src/payments/, src/api/routes/
Test search scope: tests/payments/

---

### Matrix

| Work Item | AC | AC Description | Code File / Function | Test File / Test Name | Status |
|-----------|----|----|--------|---------------------|--------|
| AB#3105 | AC1 | Payment created on Stripe and order marked paid | src/payments/StripeService.ts: chargeCard() | tests/payments/StripeService.test.ts: should create charge and update order | Covered |
| AB#3105 | AC2 | Payment failure shown to user with retry option | src/api/routes/payments.ts: handlePaymentError() | — | Code Only |
| AB#3105 | AC3 | Webhook confirms payment and triggers fulfillment | — | — | NOT COVERED |

---

### Coverage Summary

| Status | Count | % of ACs |
|--------|-------|---------|
| Covered | 1 | 33% |
| Code Only | 1 | 33% |
| NOT COVERED | 1 | 33% |
| UNGRADED | 0 | 0% |

---

### Gap Analysis

**Code Only — 1 AC**
Code exists for AC2 but no test was found for `handlePaymentError()`.
Recommend Assert.IQ `/generate-tests` for:
- AB#3105 AC2 — src/api/routes/payments.ts: handlePaymentError()

**NOT COVERED — 1 AC**
AC3 (webhook confirms payment) has no corresponding code in the scanned
directories. This is an INTENT gap.
- Action: Implement the webhook handler, or create a follow-up work item
  if this AC is intentionally deferred from this sprint.
- Owner: Backend team
- Note: AC3 is a critical path — without webhook confirmation, the order
  fulfillment system does not receive the payment signal.

---

### INTENT Signal: WEAK
One INTENT gap (NOT COVERED AC) and one QUALITY gap (Code Only AC) present.
Do not close this work item until both gaps are resolved.

@di-review-required
```

---

### Example 3: UNGRADED — Test Files Not Locatable

**Result (UNGRADED excerpt):**

```
### Matrix excerpt

| Work Item | AC | Code File / Function | Test File / Test Name | Status |
|-----------|-----|--------|---------------------|--------|
| AB#1901 | AC1 | src/auth/TokenService.ts: validateToken() | UNGRADED | UNGRADED |

---

### Gap Analysis

**UNGRADED — 1 AC**
- AB#1901 AC1: Code was found at `src/auth/TokenService.ts: validateToken()`.
  Test files could not be located — the test directory convention for this
  project is unknown and no `tests/` or `spec/` directory was found at the
  provided scope paths.
- Resolution: Provide the path to the test directory (e.g. `__tests__/`,
  `spec/`, `cypress/integration/`) and re-run this skill.
  Do not interpret UNGRADED as "no tests exist" — the data is simply unavailable.
```

---

## Governance
- Never declare a status of Covered based on code alone — the test must exist
  and must be identifiable as covering the specific AC
- UNGRADED is never equivalent to "no tests" — it means the assessment could
  not be completed; state the reason and the resolution path
- NOT COVERED ACs are INTENT gaps, not QUALITY gaps — the missing element is
  the implementation of the requirement, not the tests for existing code
- All output carries `@di-review-required` — the matrix is a draft; the team
  must verify the code-to-AC mapping is accurate before using it for audit purposes
- Test generation belongs to Assert.IQ — this skill identifies Code Only gaps
  and recommends Assert.IQ; it does not generate the tests itself
- A work item must not be closed with NOT COVERED ACs unless the team has
  explicitly deferred those ACs to a documented follow-up work item

## Related Skills
- `/review-acceptance-criteria` — review ACs before generating the matrix to
  ensure they are testable; untestable ACs produce incorrect matrix rows
- `/review-pr-readiness` — the traceability matrix is a key input to the PR
  readiness verdict; Code Only and NOT COVERED gaps affect the QUALITY signal
- `/generate-release-notes` — the coverage summary from the matrix informs the
  INTENT section of the release notes
- Assert.IQ `/generate-tests` — use to fill Code Only gaps identified in the
  matrix; Dev.IQ identifies the gaps, Assert.IQ generates the tests
